compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Plan-review triage (PASS, 20 advisories) — 2026-07-01

OAuth App created by operator; Client ID + Client Secret are in SSM at
/rebar/prod/github-oauth-client-{id,secret} (verified readable, non-CHANGEME).
AWS access restored this session; GitHub still lacks admin:org (org OAuth App was
the operator's manual step — done).

Decision: the advisories converge on one truth — the runbook over-promised SSM→config
automation the scripts don't yet do. Remediating by IMPLEMENTING that automation (not
downgrading the runbook to manual), which is the point of this ticket.

Valid → fixing in the code half of WS8:
- adv 1,2,4,6,8,10,11,12 (dup cluster): fetch-secrets.sh + compose-up.sh don't materialize
  the OAuth params. FIX: fetch both into .env; substitute client-id into gerrit.config;
  write client-secret into secure.config (0600); fail-loud if absent under auth.type=OAUTH.
- adv 3,5,9,14,17,18: install-plugins.sh only handles events-log; no pinned oauth.jar +
  sha256. FIX: add an oauth.jar install block (pinned URL + sha256, events-log discipline);
  record real artifact + sha256 in plugins/README.md (research agent resolving the 3.14
  artifact now).
- adv 15: user_data.sh PARAMS array is a second unenumerated consumer. FIX: add both params.
- adv 7: bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh uses the dev-login endpoint that dies under OAUTH.
  FIX: document post-OAUTH bootstrap (bot HTTP password) in the runbook.
- adv 16: GitHub-outage blast radius. FIX: add a break-glass section to the runbook.
- adv 19: account-migration idempotency. FIX: state the migration is idempotent (skip
  already-migrated external-ids) in the runbook.
- adv 20: no file_impact. FIX: set_file_impact recorded (8 paths).

Judged lower-priority / deferred with justification:
- adv 13 (stand up a local Docker Gerrit 3.14 spike before cutover): heavyweight. Mitigated
  by (a) compose-up.sh failing loud if oauth.jar is absent under OAUTH, and (b) the runbook
  Step-6 clean-boot + login verification BEFORE proceeding to WS7. The live install-then-verify
  IS the compatibility check; a rollback path is documented. Noted as residual risk.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Self code-review (2 finder agents + verify) — remediated 4 findings

Ran a correctness pass + a robustness/security pass on the staged diff. Cleared as safe
(verified, not bugs): set -e + `return 1` on sha-mismatch still aborts; `cut -d= -f2-`
handles `=` in values; the `type = OAUTH` grep is anchored so it never matches a comment;
cross-file var names all align. Real findings fixed:

1. **secure.config clobber (correctness).** `cat > secure.config` truncated the file every
   boot, dropping Gerrit's own keys (registerEmailPrivateKey, etc.). FIX: write both creds
   with `git config --file` — a MERGE (Gerrit configs ARE git-config files). Verified a
   pre-existing key survives.
2. **Value-metachar hazard (correctness).** sed client-id substitution + unquoted heredoc
   secret could mangle values containing | & $ ` \. FIX: `git config --file` set is
   metachar-immune. Verified: id `0v23li&weird|id` and secret `se$cret`+backtick+`\back&|`
   round-trip byte-exact.
3. **Unconditional OAuth requirement (both agents).** fetch-secrets.sh hard-required the
   OAuth creds on every boot → non-OAUTH rollback-via-compose-up broke; fresh apply died.
   FIX: fetch them via get_param_optional (blank if unpopulated); compose-up's OAUTH branch
   enforces presence only when auth.type = OAUTH is actually selected.
4. **compose-up ↔ install-plugins coupling + moving-artifact time-bomb (robustness).**
   compose-up called install-plugins.sh (network) on every boot, coupling whole-stack
   availability to GerritForge CI and the non-boot-critical events-log. FIX: install-plugins
   is a separate operator step (runbook Step 3); compose-up only VERIFIES oauth.jar present
   (fail-loud). Also reverted the user_data.sh change — those params have NO cloud-init
   consumer (containers read infra/compose/.env, not /etc/rebar/.env), so adv-15 was a
   conditional false positive; adding them only created a fresh-boot FATAL.

Plus: chmod 600 re-asserted on secure.config; `git` host-prereq guard in compose-up;
runbook Step 2 recommends create-CHANGEME-then-overwrite to avoid mid-apply
ParameterAlreadyExists. shellcheck + make lint clean; materialization functionally tested.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## WS8 LIVE CUTOVER COMPLETE + VERIFIED (2026-07-01)

Executed on the production Gerrit box (i-00880b2c7f13527c5) via AWS SSM after PR #112 merged.

### What was done
1. **Bot HTTP password** — already provisioned in S4a (SSM /rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token);
   verified working PRE-flip (HTTP 200). No action needed — DB-only service user survives OAUTH.
2. **Durable admin lever** — registered a session admin SSH key on account 1000000 (Administrators)
   via the tested bootstrap-gerrit-admin.sh (NoteDb, auth-mode-independent). Key at
   /root/.ssh/gerrit_admin on the box (root-only) — a break-glass admin path that survives OAUTH.
3. **Synced merged-main files** to the box's stale /opt/rebar (gerrit.config, compose-up.sh,
   fetch-secrets.sh, install-plugins.sh) so a later compose-up cannot revert the flip.
4. **Installed oauth.jar** via install-plugins.sh — sha256 VERIFIED on disk
   (2bcf58a652fe5e513d7a4c73362dfc5d9a3dc697f699a5280416ae6f86d0242f), 4,077,624 bytes.
5. **Flipped** via compose-up.sh: materialized client-id into gerrit.config + merged client-secret
   into secure.config (Gerrit's own registerEmailPrivateKey PRESERVED — the merge, not clobber),
   restarted gerrit. Backup at /var/gerrit/site/etc/gerrit.config.pre-ws8.bak for rollback.

### Verification (Step 6) — 3/4 mechanical checks PASS
- auth_type == OAUTH (public via nginx AND internal). ✅
- gerrit-oauth-provider plugin ENABLED (clean load in error_log). ✅
- Anonymous /a/ -> 401; /login/ -> 302 to github.com/login/oauth/authorize with the correct
  client_id (0v23liqRBRUUf7wZQqPr) + redirect_uri (…/oauth). DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT GONE. ✅
- rebar-review-bot HTTP auth still works POST-flip (HTTP 200, acct 1000002). ✅
- All plugins ENABLED; both containers Up; review-bot restarted clean (health 200, no degraded). ✅

### Remaining (needs a human)
- **"Human logs in via GitHub"** — requires interactive GitHub OAuth consent in a browser
  (can't be done headlessly). Once a human logs in, their new Gerrit account can be added to
  Administrators via admin SSH (I hold the lever). This is the last Step-6 item before closing WS8.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## WS8 COMPLETE — human GitHub login verified + admin granted (2026-07-01)

Final Step-6 items now pass:
- Human logs in via GitHub: JoeOakhartNava authenticated through the GitHub OAuth consent
  screen and landed in Gerrit as account 1000007 (joeoakhart@navapbc.com). (The earlier 404
  was a STALE pasted authorize URL with a used `state` — the client_id 0v23liqRBRUUf7wZQqPr is
  correct as stored; a fresh UI sign-in works. It's a genuine OAuth App, not a GitHub App.)
- Account 1000007 added to the Administrators group via the admin SSH lever (gerrit set-members;
  resolved by GitHub username). Verified: Administrators = {1000000 admin, 1000007 JoeOakhartNava}.

### WS8 acceptance criteria — all met
- In-repo automation (PR #112, merged): SSM slots, fetch-secrets (optional creds), compose-up
  git-config materialization, install-plugins oauth.jar (pinned+sha256), gerrit.config OAUTH flip,
  runbook reconcile. Self-reviewed (4 findings fixed).
- Live: auth_type=OAUTH (public+internal), gerrit-oauth-provider ENABLED, DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT
  gone (anon 401, /login->GitHub), bot HTTP survives (200), a human logs in via GitHub, human is admin.

### Operational notes for follow-on (WS7/WS6-b)
- Break-glass admin: a session-generated admin SSH key is at /root/.ssh/gerrit_admin on the box
  (root-only) — non-GitHub admin path per the runbook break-glass section. Rotate/replace with a
  team key at leisure.
- Rollback: /var/gerrit/site/etc/gerrit.config.pre-ws8.bak (restore + docker restart compose-gerrit-1).
- Admin account (1000000) has NO working HTTP password (SSM gerrit-admin-password != a Gerrit HTTP
  pw); admin ops go via the SSH key. Human admins now use GitHub OAuth.

compiled_state.description:
# WS8 — Harden Gerrit auth (replace DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT with GitHub OAuth)

## Why
The live Gerrit (`rebar.solutions.navateam.com`, 3.14) runs `auth.type =
DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT` — a dev-only mode where anyone can impersonate any account (no
real IdP). It CANNOT be the sole gate on production GitHub `main` (WS7) until real auth is in
place. **Confirmed strategy (2026-07-01): GitHub OAuth via the `gerrit-oauth-provider` plugin
(GitHub backend), no reverse-proxy org gate** — access curated via a short account roster + Gerrit
ACLs. Precedent: GerritHub authenticates its whole user base this way; the plugin is actively
maintained (3.13.x, tracks the Gerrit release train). Blocks WS7.

## Config (the target state)
`infra/compose/gerrit.config`:
```ini
[auth]
    type = OAUTH
    gitBasicAuthPolicy = HTTP        # bots + git-over-HTTP keep working via HTTP passwords
[plugin "gerrit-oauth-provider-github-oauth"]
    root-url = "https://github.com/"
    client-id = "<GitHub OAuth App client id>"     # non-secret; from SSM
```
`etc/secure.config` (secrets, NOT committed — materialized from SSM like the S5 deploy key):
```ini
[plugin "gerrit-oauth-provider-github-oauth"]
    client-secret = "<GitHub OAuth App client secret>"
```
Plus: install the pinned `gerrit-oauth-provider` jar into `$GERRIT_SITE/plugins`, and remove ALL
`DEVELOPMENT_*` auth.

## Deliverables — split by access boundary
### In-repo (committed; applied on a fresh redeploy) — DELIVERABLE THIS SESSION
- A runbook `infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md`: the full procedure (OAuth App, plugin,
  gerrit.config/secure.config, SSM params, redeploy, bot HTTP-password provisioning, account
  migration, verification, rollback).
- `gerrit-oauth-provider` pinned-jar provenance in `infra/gerrit/plugins/README.md` +
  install-plugins scaffolding.
- The `[auth]` block comment in `infra/compose/gerrit.config` points at the runbook + WS8 (the
  live flip to `type = OAUTH` lands with the plugin+secrets chain, per the runbook — flipping it
  in isolation would break a fresh Gerrit boot).

### Access-gated (needs org admin + working AWS) — HANDOFF
- Create the **GitHub OAuth App** on `navapbc` (callback `https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/oauth`)
  → client-id/secret. (Needs GitHub org admin via the web UI; this session's token lacks `admin:org`.)
- Put client-id/secret in **SSM** under the Gerrit secret prefix; add the params to `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`.
- **Apply to the live host** (AWS SSM/SSH — this session's AWS session is EXPIRED): install the
  plugin, seed the OAuth config into the live `site/etc/gerrit.config` + `secure.config`, remove
  become-any-account, restart Gerrit.
- **Provision bot credentials BEFORE cutover**: give the `rebar-review-bot` service account an HTTP
  password (dev-mode required none), so its git/REST access survives the auth change.

## Acceptance Criteria
### In-repo (this session)
- [ ] `infra/runbooks/gerrit-auth-hardening.md` documents the complete GitHub-OAuth hardening procedure (OAuth App → plugin → gerrit.config/secure.config → SSM → redeploy → bot passwords → account migration → verify → rollback).
- [ ] The `gerrit-oauth-provider` jar is pinned + provenance-documented (plugins/README.md) and wired into the plugin install path.
- [ ] `infra/compose/gerrit.config` `[auth]` comment points at the runbook + WS8 (records that the live flip to OAUTH lands with the plugin+secrets chain).
### Live (access-gated)
- [ ] GitHub OAuth App created; client-id/secret in SSM; `ssm.tf` params added.
- [ ] Live Gerrit switched to `auth.type = OAUTH` (become-any-account removed); Gerrit restarts healthy; a human logs in via GitHub OAuth.
- [ ] `rebar-review-bot` has an HTTP password; its REST/git access verified post-cutover.

## Dependencies
- Blocks WS7 (the Gerrit-only cutover must not precede real auth).
- Live steps need `navapbc` GitHub org admin + working AWS access to the Gerrit host (d251 infra).

file_impact.reason: add OAuth params to cloud-init fetch
materialize client-id into gerrit.config + client-secret into secure.config
flip auth.type to OAUTH + plugin block

compiled_state.title: WS8: Harden Gerrit auth (GitHub OAuth, replace DEVELOPMENT_BECOME_ANY_ACCOUNT)
